raw_log_normalization: pulled via `rtk proxy gh run view --job <id> --log` and stripped the leading <job>\t<step>\t prefix per line.
notes: Process-crash failure on numpy's reverse-sorts perf branch. test_datetime_nat_argsort_stability (test_datetime.py:226) calls argsort() on a NaT-containing datetime array; numpy's C extension segfaults; pytest's faulthandler dumps the stack and exits 245. Only smoke_test (3.12) finished — smoke_test (3.14t) was cancelled when 3.12 failed; downstream matrix legs (full, debug, custom_checks, armhf_test, benchmark, array_api_tests, sdist, all_versions) are all skipped. flaky_or_transient = false (real C-code regression on a perf branch). requires_repo_context = true (the log can pinpoint WHERE the segfault happened but not WHY without the C source). 5553 lines / 657 KB / log_size_bucket=large. Selected as Batch 3 case 1 — first v2/stress case (process-crash is an unusual evidence format that exercises the stress bucket's 'unusual evidence format' criterion). Different from any existing v2 or v1.3 case shape: no assertion diff, no compile error block; just `Fatal Python error: Segmentation fault` plus a Python traceback into a C extension.
